content: Solo/small OSS project scope and maintenance pitfalls: (1) Solo scope creep feels like craftsmanship — adding one more node, one more race branch — and is nearly impossible to self-detect. (2) Comprehensive at launch goal delays shipping indefinitely; every incomplete node is a shipping blocker. (3) Content freshness requires active editorial process — good architecture (decoupled data) is necessary but not sufficient; someone must actually update nodes after patches. (4) Community contribution without governance (clear review process, node templates, style guides) creates inconsistent quality. (5) Silence at launch is normal for OSS tools but catastrophic for motivation; no feedback loop = abandonment. (6) Build-it-and-they-will-come failure is the #1 OSS gaming tool failure mode — launch timing relative to community events (ladder seasons, patches) matters. (7) API dependency on a single external service (w3champions) creates project risk if that service changes APIs or shuts down.
